steps.title: Distribute the four
steps.content:
Start with the part of the expression that has multiplication outside the parentheses. The $4$ must be distributed to both terms inside $(x^2+7)$, so multiply $4$ by $x^2$ and by $7$:
$$4(x^2+7)=4x^2+28$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Substitute the expanded terms back into the full expression. The only like terms are $9x^2$ and $4x^2$; the $x^3$, $-5x$, and $28$ terms do not combine with anything else: $$(x^3+9x^2-5x)+(4x^2+28)=x^3+13x^2-5x+28$$ The answer is C.
